title: Introduction to JoinpointR
text: function model_jp()allows fit joinpoint regression models log-response levels one categorical variable(s). examples, going use simulated dataset HIV rates sex five regions
code: # Load required packages library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(tidyr) library(ggplot2) library(joinpointR)  # Seed for reproducibility set.seed(123)  # Generate example data data <- expand_grid(   year = 2015:2024,   sex = c("Male", "Female"),   region = c("North", "Central", "South", "East", "West") ) %>%    left_join(     tribble(       ~region   , ~slope1 , ~slope2 ,       "North"   ,      -6 ,       4 ,       "Central" ,      -5 ,       3 ,       "South"   ,       3 ,      -4 ,       "East"    ,      -7 ,       2 ,       "West"    ,       2 ,      -5     )   ) %>%    mutate(     trend = if_else(       year <= 2019,       slope1 * (year - 2015),       slope1 * 4 + slope2 * (year - 2019)     ),      hiv_rate = 100 +       trend +       if_else(sex == "Male", 10, 0) +       rnorm(n(), 0, 0.8)   ) %>%    select(year, region, sex, hiv_rate) #> Joining with `by = join_by(region)`

title: Introduction to JoinpointR
code: mod1 <- model_jp(   data = data,   value = "hiv_rate",   time = "year",   group = "sex",   k = 3,   step = TRUE,   test = TRUE ) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 .. 3 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2         3  #> -68.07563 -62.94894 -61.94894 -60.94894  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints:  0   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 .. 3 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2         3  #> -79.75981 -74.52355 -66.71104 -65.71104  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints:  0

what: Fixed number of joinpoints by sex
title: Introduction to JoinpointR
code: mod2 <- model_jp(   data = data,   value = "hiv_rate",   time = "year",   group = "sex",   k = 1,   step = FALSE,   test = FALSE )  mod2 #> $Male #> Call: segmented.lm(obj = stats::lm(formula, data = .x), seg.Z = stats::as.formula(paste0("~",  #>     time)), npsi = k) #>  #> Coefficients of the linear terms: #> (Intercept)         year      U1.year   #>    67.79592     -0.03136      0.03478   #>  #> Estimated Break-Point(s): #> psi1.year   #>      2019   #>  #> $Female #> Call: segmented.lm(obj = stats::lm(formula, data = .x), seg.Z = stats::as.formula(paste0("~",  #>     time)), npsi = k) #>  #> Coefficients of the linear terms: #> (Intercept)         year      U1.year   #>    60.95218     -0.02792      0.03045   #>  #> Estimated Break-Point(s): #> psi1.year   #>      2019

what: Stepwise joinpoint regression models by sex and region
title: Introduction to JoinpointR
code: mod3 <- model_jp(   data = data,   value = "hiv_rate",   time = "year",   group = c("sex", "region"),   step = TRUE ) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 .. #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #> 2  #> -16.61357 -65.10991 -66.50574  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 1  (1 breakpoint(s) removed due to small slope diff) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #> detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -21.18709 -63.39766 -65.07485  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 1  (1 breakpoint(s) removed due to small slope diff) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 .. #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -19.74890 -62.93346 -64.62387  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -16.45596 -61.81299 -57.25832  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 1   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -28.21633 -66.88915 -63.47310  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 1   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 .. #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #> title: Introduction to JoinpointR
text: function get_apc() provides annual percentage change 95% confidence interval models significant joinpoints (class: "segmented" "lm") function returns error significant joinpoints detected (class = "lm").
code: get_apc(mod2$Female) #>          APC          CI #> slope1 -2.8% -5.4%; 0.0% #> slope2  0.3% -2.5%; 3.1% get_apc(mod1$Female, digits = 1) #> Error in `get_apc()`: #> ! `mod` must be an object of class 'segmented'

title: Introduction to JoinpointR
text: function get_aapc() allows users estimate global trend even significant joinpoints detected. can choose show significance stars instead CI:
code: get_aapc(mod1$Female, digits = 1, show_ci = TRUE) #> [1] "-1.1% (-2.0%; -0.1%)" get_aapc(mod1$Female, digits = 1, show_ci = FALSE) #> [1] "-1.1% *"

title: Introduction to JoinpointR
code: # id: tab-1 mod2 |>   summary_jp(digits = 1, var1 = "Sex", ft = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   Sex       JP Period      APC CI        AAPC    #>   <chr>  <dbl> <chr>     <dbl> <chr>     <chr>   #> 1 Male       1 2015-2019  -3.1 -6.1; 0   -1.2% * #> 2 Male      NA 2019-2024   0.3 -2.8; 3.6 NA      #> 3 Female     1 2015-2019  -2.8 -5.4; 0   -1.1% * #> 4 Female    NA 2019-2024   0.3 -2.5; 3.1 NA

title: Introduction to JoinpointR
text: Sex JP Period APC CI AAPC Male 1 2015-2019 -3.1 -6.1; 0 -1.2% *  2019-2024 0.3 -2.8; 3.6  Female 1 2015-2019 -2.8 -5.4; 0 -1.1% *  2019-2024 0.3 -2.5; 3.1  * p < 0.05JP: number joinpoints; APC: annual percent change; CI: 95% confidence interval; AAPC: average annual percent change (95% CI).
code: # id: tab-2 mod2 |>   summary_jp(digits = 1, var1 = "Sex", ft = TRUE)

what: HTML Tables by two grouping variables
title: Introduction to JoinpointR
code: mod3 |>   summary_jp(digits = 1, var1 = "Region", var2 = "Sex", ft = TRUE) mod3 |>   summary_jp(digits = 1, var1 = "Región", var2 = "Sexo", ft = TRUE, lan = "es")

title: Introduction to JoinpointR
text: Split sex  Split region  Hide data points  Hide joinpoint(s) marker(s)  Change colors legend position using ggplot2 functions  Change facet layout
code: mod1 |>   gg_jpoint() mod1 |>   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE) mod3 |>   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE) mod3 |>   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE, obs = FALSE) mod3 |>   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE, jp = FALSE) mod3 %>%   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE, jp = FALSE) +   scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Sex:Region") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") mod3 %>%   gg_jpoint(facets = TRUE) +   facet_wrap(~group, ncol = 5) +   scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Sex:Region") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tamara Ricardo. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ricardo T (2026). joinpointR: Tidy Tools Joinpoint Regression Models. R package version 0.6.2, https://github.com/datos-ine/joinpointR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {joinpointR: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models},   author = {Tamara Ricardo},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.6.2},   url = {https://github.com/datos-ine/joinpointR}, }

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: El objetivo de joinpointR es ajustar modelos de regresión joinpoint por grupos y generar resúmenes en formato tidy del Cambio Porcentual Anual (APC) y del Cambio Porcentual Anual Promedio (AAPC), facilitando el análisis de tendencias en estudios epidemiológicos.

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: goal joinpointR fit joinpoint regression models groups generate tidy summaries Annual Percent Change (APC) Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC), facilitating trend analysis epidemiological studies.

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: can install development version GitHub / Podés instalar la versión en desarrollo desde GitHub: Repository: https://github.com/datos-ine/joinpointR
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("datos-ine/joinpointR")

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: package provides simple reproducible workflow / El paquete propone un flujo simple y reproducible: Fit joinpoint models group / Ajustar modelos joinpoint por grupo Extract APC segment / Extraer APC por segmento Compute AAPC / Calcular AAPC Generate summary tables / Generar tablas resumen

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: model_jp() → fit joinpoint models group / ajusta modelos joinpoint por grupo get_apc() → extract APC segment / extrae APC por segmento get_aapc() → compute AAPC / computa AAPC summary_jp() → generate summary tables (tibble flextable) / genera tablas resumen (tibble o flextable)

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
code: library(joinpointR) library(dplyr)  df <- tibble(   year = rep(2000:2010, 2),   rate = c(runif(11, 10, 20), runif(11, 5, 15)),   group = rep(c("Male", "Female"), each = 11) )  mods <- model_jp(   data = df,   value = "rate",   time = "year",   group = "group",   step = TRUE )  # APC (only works when class segmented lm) get_apc(mods$Male, digits = 1, time = "year", dec = ".") # Will generate an error  get_apc(mods$Female, digits = 1, time = "year", dec = ".")  # AAPC with 95% CI get_aapc(mods$Male, show_ci = TRUE)  # AAPC with significance stars get_aapc(mods$Male, show_ci = FALSE)  # Summary Table summary_jp(mods)

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: Returns table ready reporting (e.g., Word) using flextable. / Devuelve una tabla lista para exportar Word o informes mediante flextable.
code: # English (default) summary_jp(mods, ft = TRUE, lan = "en", var1 = "Sex")  # Spanish summary_jp(mods, ft = TRUE, lan = "es", var1 = "Sexo")

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: generated table includes / La tabla generada incluye: Number joinpoints / Número de joinpoints (JP) Time periods segment / Períodos de cada segmento APC per segment / por segmento 95% Confidence intervals / Intervalos de confianza al 95% AAPC (global tendency / tendencia global)

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
code: # Plot results mods |>   gg_joinpoint(obs = TRUE, jp = TRUE, facets = FALSE)  # Facets by group mods |>   gg_jpoint(obs = TRUE, jp = TRUE, facets = TRUE)  # Hide observed mods |>   gg_jpoint(obs = FALSE, jp = TRUE, facets = FALSE)  # Hide joinpoints mods |>   gg_jpoint(obs = TRUE, jp = FALSE, facets = FALSE)

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: package uses / El paquete utiliza: segmented fitting jointpoint regression models / para regresión joinpoint dplyr, purrr, tidyr, tibble data management / para manipulación de datos ggplot2 plotting results / para graficar resultados flextable summary tables / para tablas formateadas

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: response variable log-transformed / La variable respuesta se transforma logarítmicamente Model selection based Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) / La selección de modelos se basa en el Criterio de Información Bayesiano (BIC) step = FALSE fits joinpoint regression model number joinpoints specified k/ Cuando step = FALSE ajusta una regresión joinpoint para el número de joinpoints especificados en k. Results returned tidy format / Los resultados se devuelven en formato tidy para facilitar su uso en análisis reproducibles Formatted tables optional / La creación de tablas formateadas es opcional (ft = TRUE)

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: MIT License

title: Tidy Tools for Joinpoint Regression Models
text: Tamara Ricardo Instituto Nacional de Epidemiología (INE), Argentina ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0921-2611

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
text: Estimates Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) 95% confidence interval. Optionally displays statistical significance using significance stars.

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
code: get_aapc(mod, digits = 1, show_ci = TRUE, dec = ".")

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
text: mod Joinpoint regression model (segmented object) linear regression model (lm object). digits Number decimal places display (integer). show_ci Logical; TRUE, displays 95% confidence interval. FALSE, displays significance stars. dec Character. Decimal separator use (e.g., "." ",").

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
text: character string AAPC either 95% confidence interval significance stars.

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
text: Tamara Ricardo

title: Average Annual Percent Change (AAPC) — get_aapc
code: # Load example data data("plant", package = "segmented")  names(plant) #> [1] "y"     "time"  "group"  # Fit the joinpoint models mods <- model_jp(data = plant, value = "y", time = "time", group = "group", k = 2, test = TRUE) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>  -10.65677  -86.81612 -107.18311  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #>  30.10898 -57.99211 -73.23987  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>   1.713582 -66.784879 -73.378431  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2    # AAPC of the first model get_aapc(mods$RKW, digits = 1, show_ci = TRUE, dec = ".") #> [1] "0.1% (0.1%; 0.1%)"

what: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
text: Calculates Annual Percent Change (APC) segment 95% confidence interval.

title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
code: get_apc(mod, digits = 1, time = "time", dec = ".")

title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
text: mod Joinpoint regression model (segmented object). digits Number decimal places display (integer). time Time variable used model (character). dec Character. Decimal separator use (e.g., "." ",").

title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
text: character vector APC 95% CI segment.

title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
text: Tamara Ricardo

title: Annual Percent Change by Segment (APC) — get_apc
code: # Load example data data("plant", package = "segmented")  names(plant) #> [1] "y"     "time"  "group"  # Fit the joinpoint models mods <- model_jp(data = plant, value = "y", time = "time", group = "group", k = 2, test = TRUE) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>  -10.65677  -86.81612 -107.18311  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #>  30.10898 -57.99211 -73.23987  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>   1.713582 -66.784879 -73.378431  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2    # Obtain APC (95% CI) get_apc(mods$RKW, digits = 1, time = "time", dec = ".") #>          APC          CI #> slope1  0.5%  0.4%; 0.5% #> slope2  0.1%  0.1%; 0.2% #> slope3 -0.1% -0.1%; 0.0%

title: Plot Joinpoint Regression Models — gg_jpoint
text: Creates ggplot showing observed values, fitted joinpoint regression lines, optional joinpoints.

title: Plot Joinpoint Regression Models — gg_jpoint
code: gg_jpoint(mods, obs = TRUE, jp = TRUE, facets = FALSE)

title: Plot Joinpoint Regression Models — gg_jpoint
text: mods List joinpoint regression models (output model_jp()). obs Logical. TRUE, displays observed data points. jp Logical. TRUE, displays joinpoints vertical dashed lines. facets Logical. TRUE, displays one panel per group using facets.

title: Plot Joinpoint Regression Models — gg_jpoint
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Joinpoint Regression Models — gg_jpoint
code: library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union # Load example data data("plant", package = "segmented")  names(plant) #> [1] "y"     "time"  "group"  # Fit the joinpoint models mods <- model_jp(data = plant, value = "y", time = "time", group = "group", k = 2, test = TRUE) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>  -10.65677  -86.81612 -107.18311  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #>  30.10898 -57.99211 -73.23987  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary. Increase 'Kmax'? #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>          0          1          2  #>   1.713582 -66.784879 -73.378431  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 2    # Plot results gg_jpoint(mods, obs = TRUE, jp = TRUE, facets = FALSE)   # Facets by group gg_jpoint(mods, obs = TRUE, jp = TRUE, facets = TRUE)

what: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
text: Fits segmented linear regression models groups age-standardized rates using joinpoint regression. Models can fitted using either stepwise selection procedure based Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) fixed number joinpoints. Internally calls segmented::selgmented() segmented::segmented() applies log transformation response variable.

title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
code: model_jp(data, value, time, group, k = 2, step = TRUE, test = TRUE)

title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
text: data Data frame containing age-standardized rates. value Name response variable (character). time Name time variable (character). group Name one grouping variables (character vector). k Maximum number joinpoints estimated (integer). step Use stepwise procedure select number joinpoints (logical). test whether test differences slope t-values stepwise selection procedure. used step = TRUE.

title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
text: list models group.

title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
text: Tamara Ricardo

title: Joinpoint Regression Models by Groups — model_jp
code: # Generate example data library(dplyr) df <- mtcars |> mutate(   year = seq(2000, length.out = n(), by = 1),   group = factor(paste("cyl", cyl, sep = "_")),   rate = mpg ) |> select(year, group, rate)  # Check group levels levels(df$group) #> [1] "cyl_4" "cyl_6" "cyl_8"  # Fit models mods <- model_jp(data = df, value = "rate", time = "year", group = "group",  k = 2, step = TRUE, test = TRUE) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -2.347654 -2.027073  2.417460  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints:  0   #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -12.44537 -15.72914 -14.72914  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints: 0  (1 breakpoint(s) removed due to small slope diff) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #>  #> BIC to detect no. of breakpoints: #>         0         1         2  #> -1.227750 -0.614267  3.606649  #>  #> No. of selected breakpoints:  0    # Show the output of the first model mods$cyl_6 #>  #> Call: #> stats::lm(formula = formula, data = .x) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)         year   #>   -3.820829     0.003514   #>  summary(mods$cyl_6) #>  #> Call: #> stats::lm(formula = formula, data = .x) #>  #> Residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -0.25210 -0.09738 -0.03658  0.12882  0.25009  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|) #> (Intercept) -3.820829  12.032563  -0.318    0.758 #> year         0.003514   0.005962   0.589    0.570 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.1734 on 9 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.03716,	Adjusted R-squared:  -0.06982  #> F-statistic: 0.3473 on 1 and 9 DF,  p-value: 0.5701 #>

what: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
text: Generates table displaying number joinpoints, time breaks, APC 95% confidence interval, AAPC statistical significance list joinpoint models.

title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
code: summary_jp(   mods,   digits = 1,   var1 = "group",   var2 = "subgroup",   ft = FALSE,   lan = c("en", "es") )

title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
text: mods List joinpoint regression models (model_jp() output). digits Number decimal places display (integer). var1 Character. Name grouping variable. var2 Character. Name subgrouping variable (optional). ft Logical. TRUE returns flextable object, FALSE returns tibble. lan Language output: "en" (English) "es" (Spanish).

title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
text: tibble flextable object.

title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
text: Tamara Ricardo

title: Summary Table For Joinpoint Regression Models — summary_jp
code: library(dplyr) # Load example data data("plant", package = "segmented")  names(plant) #> [1] "y"     "time"  "group"  # Fit the joinpoint models mods <- model_jp(data = plant, value = "y", time = "time", group = "group", k = 2, test = TRUE) #> No. of breakpoints: 2 ..  #> Warning: The best BIC value at the boundary.
